chapters.title: Humanity is designed to create and innovate, and this is integral to our growth
chapters.text:
Creativity is an important part of our advancement. Aside from this, it is also a quality of human innovation, the literal driving force behind it. Without creativity, we’d get stuck with recurring problems that lack practical solutions. But, thanks to evolution, there is a way to prevent these events from occurring. The human mind has adapted to be creative, and over time, this skill has gotten sharper.

The human mind has been equipped with the ability to devise workable solutions literally out of thin air through innovative thinking.

By definition, creativity is the skill of an individual to create, perform, or think of something in a never-before-seen method. This means, for every creative action, something is made or done in a new way. The ability to always find new and fresh ways to generate ideas and solutions is a much-needed skill today. Creativity is the fuel for dreams to get actualized. Without it, many of our life goals will go unfulfilled.

Creativity combines neatly with innovative thinking and helps us achieve dreams and goals we have set for ourselves.

Many success stories are told without giving proper acknowledgment to the creative portion of it all, so people often don't see what goes on behind the scenes. Know it today, every success story starts in the mind, the seat of creativity. This process often involves a lot of visualization and experimentation. Creativity can be considered outlandish or unorthodox, but that’s the essence; if more people strayed from the conventional and let their minds wander, they’d create bolder and better outcomes.

Invention is typically born out of necessity, but it can lack innovation. If every idea tagged as “new” are actually old recycled ones, there would be no forward movement or progress. Creativity aids improvement because it offers us more unique and successful ideas to tackle current and future hurdles. A creative mind sees solutions where others see problems; when such a mind gets faced with a challenge, it creates clever and effective workarounds.

Did you know? Pixar changes movies to be relatable in different countries. For example, the title of Ellie’s adventure book in the movie “UP” appears in the various languages of the intended country it is being aired in; French, Japanese, Thai, etc.

chapters.title: The creative process and how to truly unlock the potential for creativity in everyone
chapters.text:
When Ed Catmull speaks of creativity, he regards it as unlike the traditional definition; he says creativity can see unexpected connections between unrelated concepts and ideas. At Pixar, creativity is their watchword, and this is thanks to the mind of Catmull; he marshaled the employees into a mindset that not only favored hard workers but it also rewarded and encouraged creativity.

Creativity is the natural ability to formulate ideas and solutions from the most unexpected of sources.

Catmull had a process that he operated by, one that he shares with his staff and partners. The process is pivotal to the successes that he and Pixar enjoyed right from its first movie, Toy Story, in 1995. Catmull believed that perfection was a myth and that it is in our pursuit for it; we often miss out on doing anything.

For creativity to flourish, there needs to be a supportive and constraint-free environment.

Creativity can only operate in a free environment, one that doesn’t stifle the process. The creative process is as such:
• Create. The creative process starts with creation; the idea or draft must first get formulated. This is a significant step, for without it, there’s no process.
• Learn. This step follows after the “create” step because when we formulate ideas, they often teach us things about ourselves and the idea we are attempting to bring to life. Learning involves research as well, without which we cannot explore all the possibilities the concept has to offer.
• Revise. This might get penned as the last stage but isn’t; the “revise” phase involves coming to terms that while your idea seems to have reached its “finished” phase, it can be better. There are still things you can tweak to improve the final product. This step leads us back to the “create” and “learn” phase and back to the “revise” phase as many times as is needed to get an acceptable version of the product.

Creativity is a continuous process that involves a lot of fine-tuning and learning.

Catmull believes there’s no such thing as the best version of an idea or product, only the “most recent worse version.”

When it comes to creative inspiration, job titles and hierarchy are meaningless. ~ Ed Catmull

chapters.title: Creativity is a learned skill; you can acquire it through practice
chapters.text:
Creativity is a revered skill that is explored by individuals seeking employment. It is also essential if you want to make your mark in the world. For dreamers and trendsetters, your best ally is a creative mind. Often, it is thought that one must be born with this as a gift; but this isn’t the case.

Creativity gives individuals a much-needed edge in the very competitive labor market space.

Despite some opinions, creativity isn’t a talent you are born with. In fact, it is something you can learn and perfect. Ed Catmull and other artists like himself learned to draw and kept at it until he got good at it. The creativity of his work isn’t down to a sudden burst of inspiration; it is down to a conscious and constant effort to perfect the skill.

One can learn to cultivate and master creativity through constant practice; it isn’t something you are born with.

Creativity isn’t limited to art. In any field you wish to gain mastery and be creative, the trick is to practice. A saying by former French international footballer Thierry Henry goes: “Amateurs call it genius, masters call it practice.” It gives voice to the thought process of Ed Catmull, who encourages cultivating a habit of practicing.

If you wish to gain mastery of a craft, you need to create a lifestyle built around creativity.

To be successful and stand out in whatever field you find yourself in, you must also be committed to the creative process. It should become a part of your daily routine if you wish to be good at it. So, familiarize yourself with your craft by committing to a life of practice, reinvent and perfect it.

Creativity thrives better when the ideas are unique, ensure not to copy your muses wholly.

There is, however, a drawback to avoid when embarking on the creative process—the tendency to copy others. While it is essential to emulate and seek inspiration from others who have experienced success in your field of choice, you must try not to make their style your own; it must only inspire you.

Did you know? Toy Story 2 was almost accidentally deleted.

chapters.title: There is a need for adequate management to help lead a creative team
chapters.text:
Creativity is an integral part of any business or project. A business led by a creative manager is always going to generate new and fresh ideas. For example, Take Apple for example. They’ve made it their brand to lead the software and tech industry with innovative and cutting-edge designs.

Creativity is every manager’s trusted ally when it comes to embarking on projects; it guarantees success.

But it isn’t enough to have a creative leader; members of the firm must also have that creative knack. To do this, you need to hire interested applicants who possess a creative mind and share in the firm’s goals. This way, it is much easier to get more tasks completed in little time. A good manager is not the most creative one; they are the one who recruits people with the same or possibly a higher level of creativity than theirs. They can also adequately manage an innovative team, giving them the room they need to create, learn, and revise freely.

A creative team can only be as good as its manager; a good manager feeds and rewards innovative thinking.

Creativity is a process, and it requires the correct elements for it to flow freely and thrive. It is primarily the responsibility of the manager to create favorable conditions. Ed Catmull insists that managing a creative team requires a lot of aspects. Trust in the group is essential; you must trust them to handle tasks you give them, no matter how difficult it might be.

Growth will come with failures and mistakes; the creative is about constantly perfecting the product, seeing a chance always to improve.

As a manager, you must recognize that failure is not a deterrent but a sign that you have begun your creative journey into new and untested territory. Often, mistakes are a sign that you are trying out new methods. The goal is to learn from mistakes and perfect the process to avoid more errors. From the creative process, the place of learning and refining the efforts is integral to creativity. A good manager must show faith in their team’s capability and reward innovation as well.

Failure isn’t a necessary evil. It isn’t evil at all. It is a necessary consequence of doing something new. ~ Ed Catmull

chapters.title: The creative process requires a good and well-chosen innovative team
chapters.text:
The role of a manager gets overlooked because usually, the glory is attributed to the talents in the team. The creative team might have all the talent and achieve much of anything they attempt, but it would be all for naught without a good manager. In the case of the creative team itself, it isn’t a self-creating entity, so a manager needs to assemble one first.

A good manager must possess the ability to spot creative talent and recruit them as well.

The creative team is another critical aspect, making up for a massive chunk of the production process. A good manager must have an eye for talent, get involved in the recruitment process, look out for hiring people more innovative than they are, and do all of this without letting their insecurities interfere.

Managing varying talents in a creative team is a quality a good manager should possess.

Once you have recruited these individuals, you must exude confidence, and the team will follow a sound and assured leader in their decisions. Even if the decisions end up being right, a good leader must have another plan just in case.

Team management is essential because regardless of the talents, members cannot come together without a leader.

To lead correctly is to show from the bottom, that is, working directly with your subordinates. When you create a healthy line of communication, you encourage more healthy feedback and boost productivity. If you distance yourself from your team, you cause a break in the creative process and risk ruining the final product.

If you eliminate the constraints of the traditional workplace hierarchy, you give room to a lot of creative thinking.

Pixar operates like a family, always unafraid to speak to whomever, regardless of their position or rank. Ed Catmull made it such that there are no restrictions with the chain of command. By doing so, he reduced the apprehension that comes with the usual hierarchy in companies. This “change” in the norm is just what Catmull was known for, often formulating newer and bolder means to operate the traditional production process.

chapters.title: Creativity must be a commitment and a lifestyle to avoid complacency
chapters.text:
There are further steps to take as a manager or an individual involved for the creative process to flow freely. There has to firstly be room for change and “randomness” in the process. This is because we tend to get strict and stick to our comfortable methods as we attain success.

It is easy to get entangled in complacency when we succeed. To avoid it, engage in new challenges.

Randomness does not happen in the absence of regular activity; it occurs amidst our everyday routine. This quality that randomness possesses makes it seem invisible and inaccessible. But if you wish to create new and cutting-edge ideas, you must quiet your everyday life and find the randomness in it.

It is harmful to get stuck in a pattern, and the fix is to open yourself up to randomness.

We might be actively fighting against creativity by no fault of our own. Creativity cannot occur in a strict and patterned setup, but the human mind gets tailored to find patterns, especially those that work. It is normal to find comfort in the successes we enjoy and not want to change anything to avoid losing the streak.

We must always be open to learning, strategizing, and starting afresh; there is no such thing as perfect or finished.

Patterns are limiting, and we must do our best to avoid trapping ourselves into redundant routines. Individuals and companies alike should be open to learning, and the creative process is heavily dependent on understanding and revision. Refusing to learn and change creates a limit. It would be counterproductive to set boundaries for yourself if you wish to thrive and excel.

Whatever challenges we face, we must commit ourselves to maintain our resolve and continue to soldier on regardless.

Remember not to allow any obstacles to the creative process that require a different emotional intensity than the one committed to the work. However, if you face significant obstacles, don't let them reduce the intensity of your passion. It's your passion that will see you through obstacles. A commitment to always maintain a steady level of emotional intensity will make sure you are constantly firing on all cylinders regardless of the challenge or task at hand.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Creativity is the mother of all innovation; it is humankind's ability to create never-before-seen images or ideas out of thin air. It occurs in the mind, a mental process often misconstrued as a talent gifted to only a few people. But this isn’t the case; it is, in fact, a skill that can be learned and mastered by anyone.

An excellent way to cultivate and nurture creativity is through consistent and intentional practice. Catmull mentioned his early days when he was still practicing and sketching, how he learned to draw the right way through the training he was committed to. Creativity isn’t an isolated incident; it is innate in every person. This means that one can assemble a team of creative thinkers and accomplish great things.

The person charged with assembling talents to form a creative team is called a manager, and their job isn’t only in recruitment but also in management to utilize them to their fullest potential. Without a sound and confident manager, the creative team might face challenges applying their skills to good use.

It is important to remember to treat members of your creative team with trust, respect, and the benefit of the doubt. Mistakes and failures are not definitive but indicative of new territory. As managers and individuals, the place of error is one of regrouping and learning. When you fail, use the chance to learn.

Craft is what we are expected to know; art is the unexpected use of our craft. ~ Ed Catmull

Try this

Pick up a craft or skill you're interested in and commit portions of your day to practice and perfect it. Here's a simple way to go about that:
• Decide on the time you will spend practicing daily and stick to it.
• You could start with 30-45 minutes of daily practice. More important than the time allotment is that you're patient and make consistency a priority.

Read it till the end?
